question_text: During respiration in your body, glucose is broken down to release energy. Which of the following best describes what happens to the chemical energy stored in glucose?

options.label: A
options.text: It is destroyed and disappears completely

options.label: B
options.text: It is transferred to ATP and heat energy that your body uses

options.label: C
options.text: It is converted to light energy for seeing

options.label: D
options.text: It stays stored in the glucose until you exercise

correct_answer: B) It is transferred to ATP and heat energy that your body uses
explanation: Respiration releases the chemical energy from glucose, which cells use to make ATP (the body's energy currency) and heat to keep you warm. Energy is never destroyed—it is only transferred or transformed into different forms.
